q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is pickle.loads() on user-controlled data, which enables arbitrary code execution (RCE) upon deserialization.

mock_guidance.target: Mock pickle.loads to intercept the deserialization of base64.b64decode(encoded_user) on line 84.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the raw bytes argument passed to pickle.loads. Verify that this bytes value originates from the untrusted request.POST['user'] input without any sanitization or HMAC validation.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe dummy object (e.g., a simple namespace or dict) that satisfies subsequent usage, allowing the function to continue and reach the redirect('/login') return without triggering actual deserialization.
notes: Must mock pickle.loads rather than base64.b64decode so the base64 decoding still occurs, preserving the end-to-end data flow evidence.
Ensure the mock does not suppress the finding; the violation is the call itself reaching the sink with tainted data.
